content:
Character: Chen Guoliang
Perception: Mr. Chen is solid and quiet — short, strong hands scarred by years of cooking, a face that looks like it was carved by work rather than time. His food is honest and extraordinary. I eat at Golden Autumn twice a week and he always adds something extra — a soup, a plate of greens — and refuses to charge. He reminds me of my grandfather in Algiers, the same silent generosity. I think he sees me as another young person far from home who needs feeding. I know his wife died and he runs the restaurant alone. I respect him enormously and I always pay full price even when he waves me off.
summary:
Character: Chen Guoliang
Perception: Mr. Chen is solid and quiet — short, strong hands scarred by years of cooking, a face that looks like it was carved by work rather than time. His food is honest and extraordinary. I eat at Golden Autumn twice a week and he always adds something extra — a soup, a plate of greens — and refuses to charge. He reminds me of my grandfather in Algiers, the same silent generosity. I think he sees me as another young person far from home who needs feeding. I know his wife died and he runs the restaurant alone. I respect him enormously and I always pay full price even when he waves me off.
